name: OWASP Cybersecurity Check Agent
summary: Reviews user-provided project context and delivers a high-level OWASP-aligned security check without requiring code changes.
description: OWASP Cybersecurity Check Agent helps users perform a practical, high-level application security review using files, IDE context, repository context, or CI-provided artifacts. It is designed for no-code or low-code usage and produces structured findings, likely risks, missing controls, and remediation priorities aligned to OWASP-style categories.
use_cases: Review a repository at a high level for likely OWASP risks
Assess codebase snippets or architecture notes directly in chat
Check IDE context during implementation for obvious security gaps
Review pull request or build context in a CI workflow
Create a lightweight security checklist before release
conversation_starters: Review this repository for high-level OWASP risks.
Check these files for likely authentication, authorization, and secret-management issues.
Use my IDE context to spot high-priority security gaps before I ship.
Generate an OWASP-style release readiness review from this project context.
limitations: Does not replace a formal penetration test or manual code audit
Cannot verify runtime behavior unless the user provides evidence
May miss issues outside the supplied context
Should not be treated as legal, compliance, or certification advice
